summary: Jane booking is a reserve->confirm transaction, availability keys on 'date' not 'start_date', and appointment fields are nested; a regen must preserve these live-verified contracts, not the naive single-POST guesses.
reason: Reverse-engineered from Jane's booking SPA and verified with a real booking: POST /api/v2/reservations {reservation:{...,browser_session_id}} then POST /api/v2/appointments/{id}/book (patient auto-associates when logged in, CSRF from page meta). /api/v2/openings ignores start_date and requires 'date'. appointments payload nests staff_member.professional_name and has no status field (derive from cancelled_at). Native ICS feed is /ical/<token> from the account page.
validated_outcome: Booked a real appointment end-to-end via reserve->confirm and confirmed it appears in appointments upcoming. Availability with date= returns correct future openings matching the web. calendar --out generates valid ICS incl. the new booking; calendar --url returns the /ical/<token> subscribe URL.
